concept.description: Taco Bell Express — smaller-format quick-service Mexican food units (Express Units, Power Pumpers, In-Lines) situated in non-traditional locations such as colleges, airports, gas stations, dual-brand facilities
concept.menu: Inexpensively priced, quality Mexican-style food for take-out and on-premises eating; full or limited Taco Bell menu as determined by franchisor

royalty.definition_of_gross_sales: All payments received for sales and services excluding sales taxes, employee meals, overrings and refunds
royalty.payment_timing: By 5th business day after accounting period

advertising_fund.note: No ad fund contribution required. No licensee advertising cooperatives. Franchisor has no obligation to advertise in your area.

transfer_fee.entity_restructure: $2,500 (or 3rd party rate if LA changes needed)

fees.successor_fee: Greater of $11,250 or 1/2 current initial license fee, plus required remodel
fees.liquidated_damages: Greater of 11% of last 12 months Gross Sales or $100,000
fees.late_charges: Lesser of 18% per annum or max NY law, plus admin charge

existing_units.note: Or more. Total purchase price may exceed $1,800,000 excluding real property.

territory.description: Non-exclusive license for single specified location. No territorial protection. Franchisor and YUM affiliates (KFC, Pizza Hut, HBG) can open anywhere without regard to proximity.
territory.channel_restrictions: No right to sell pre-packaged food under Trademarks. No delivery/sale outside Unit without written consent. Franchisor retains exclusive right to pre-packaged products through all channels.

term_and_renewal.successor_policy: Discretionary, subject to modification or cancellation at any time
term_and_renewal.successor_requirements: Operational/financial approval, Unit upgrade (offset/scrape-rebuild/major remodel), successor fee, sign release

termination.by_licensee: Treated as default; triggers liquidated damages (greater of $100,000 or 11% of last 12 months Gross Sales)
termination.by_franchisor_without_cause: If key fee/trademark provisions declared invalid/unenforceable
termination.non_curable_defaults: Untrained manager, denial of access, unauthorized transfer, loss of possession, felony, misrepresentation, bankruptcy, repeated defaults
termination.post_termination: De-identify Unit, stop using Trademarks/System, pay liquidated damages if applicable, franchisor may require assignment of interest

noncompete.during_term: No interest in restaurant selling Mexican-style food (except ≤10% publicly traded). Applies to licensee, immediate family, employees, shareholders.
noncompete.post_term: Same restriction for 1 year after termination due to breach, within 10-mile radius of Unit
noncompete.relationship_agreement: Broader: no interest in QSR, Mexican casual dining, or Mexican quick casual dining (during term and 1 year post)

dispute_resolution.forum: Federal or state courts in Orange County, California

litigation.note: No litigation required to be disclosed in Item 3

item19.note: Franchisor does not make any representations about future or past financial performance of any company-owned or licensed Express Units.

item21.note: SPV structure with virtually zero operating expenses. All operational costs borne by TBC under management agreement. Revenue in thousands.
